sources.name: WRAP
sources.dataset: Household food and drink waste measurement

metadata.methodology: Total food waste covers all stages of the supply chain: household, manufacturing, retail, hospitality and food service, and farm gate (where data available). Household waste (approx 70% of total by weight) is estimated via diary studies, compositional analysis of waste streams, and bin surveys. The £19bn household cost and £700 per household figures are WRAP estimates based on 2023 food prices. The 18% reduction since 2007 is calculated against WRAP's 2007 baseline estimate of 11.5 Mt.
knownIssues: WRAP's food waste measurement methodology was revised in 2018, creating a break in the time series — pre- and post-2018 figures are not directly comparable.
Farm gate and supply chain waste estimates carry higher uncertainty than household estimates due to limited mandatory reporting.
COVID-19 affected waste patterns in 2020 (more home cooking, less hospitality waste) creating a non-typical data point.
